person.name: Cristher Castro
person.bio: Cristher Castro, co-founder and COO of Cuemby, is a visionary leader and operational dynamo who drives community building in the Latam tech scene. She co-created, along with her team, the first Kubernetes Community Days in Colombia. This event paved the way for the expansion of Cloud Native Groups throughout the region. Cristher's foresight and operational expertise have been fundamental to the success of both Cuemby and the Cloud Native Latam Group. Recognizing the region's need for in-depth knowledge of cloud-native applications, Cristher championed the LFS250 (Linux Foundation Course) and administered the KCNA certifications donated by CNCF to the region. These two initiatives heightened awareness of cloud-native technologies and certifications, fortifying local proficiency in open-source solutions. She continues to promote and advocate for the recognition of the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) in the region, addressing both business and community aspects. Cristher's leadership and operational prowess exemplify her unwavering commitment to fostering a thriving Latam tech community. https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-latin-america/ https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-medellin/ https://www.cncf.io/reports/kcd-colombia-2023/ https://www.cuemby.com/about-us https://www.linkedin.com/company/fundaci%C3%B3n-hispana-de-cloud-native/?viewAsMember=true https://community.cncf.io/cloud-native-colombia/ https://www.linkedin.com/showcase/kcd-colombia/?viewAsMember=truehttps://www.linkedin.com/showcase/kcd-colombiaWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: Becoming a CNCF ambassador empowers me to further my dedication to community growth in the LATAM region. I deeply believe that the region's success hinges on the symbiotic collaboration of professionals, students, and companies. It's this harmony that forms the core of my mission: to cultivate a thriving community ecosystem centered around open-source and cloud-native technologies. Through this role, I am positioned to amplify awareness about these technologies and the transformative opportunities they introduce. My operational and logistics expertise is set to facilitate more meetups and events, thus kindling the formation of new communities. Collaborating with fellow leaders and ambassadors provides a unique platform to unite our efforts, ensuring we collectively advance our shared vision.

My homelab demo showcases full CI/CD pipelines, data pipelines, secure infrastructure-as-code practices, and end-to-end cloud-native applications. For a live demo reach me at https://www.linkedin.com/in/pietro-cloud-engineer/  or check my blog at https://blog.pietro.app

person.name: Sandesh Parasram Pawar

I specialize in Kubernetes, Helm, and CI/CD optimization to deliver faster, more reliable deployments. My passion lies in simplifying complex systems and improving efficiency through smart automation.

person.name: Shujauddin Mazhar Mohammad
person.bio: I am a seasoned Cloud DevOps engineer and I have been in IT for over 13 years now working with multiple state government/other clients from healthcare and financial domains.  I specialize in designing and managing scalable solutions across both public and private clouds. My expertise encompasses CI/CD pipelines, container orchestration with Kubernetes, infrastructure/application deployment automation using GitOps principles, implementation and integration of latest tools and technologies. Adhering to the "as code" principle, I ensure all configurations are version-controlled and reproducible, enhancing consistency and collaboration. With a strong emphasis on security, I ensure robust and efficient deployment processes that safeguard our systems while driving innovation and operational excellence via automation. I love open source technologies, CNCF projects and with a peaked interest in AI/ML, you'll find me exploring the latest tech and trends in the industry. I believe there is always some room to improve and I keep pushing my limits everyday.

In summary, I'm an engineer which means I like solving problems and helping others.

My expertise lies in transforming traditional applications into scalable and efficient cloud-native architectures. My work is instrumental in driving digital transformation and optimizing performance in the new cloud environment.

person.name: Filip Kołodziejczyk
person.bio: I'm a DevOps with a programming background. In my free time, I enjoy learning new tech stuff. I'm driven by designing and implementing complete systems that are both optimized and satisfy stakeholder needs.

person.name: Jan Melén
person.bio:
Jan Melén is an expert in networking, open-source software, and cloud technologies, with a career spanning over two decades. He is a staunch advocate for Free and Open-Source Software (FOSS) adoption within Ericsson and the broader community he engages with.

Jan has served as a Research Leader for Cloud Research, driving innovations in cloud computing. Since 2019, he has led a team dedicated to CNCF open-source projects, contributing significantly to Ericsson's Kubernetes distribution, and fostering an "upstream-first" culture within the company. He now oversees Ericsson’s open-source contributions and coordinates collaborations with CNCF projects. With his technical expertise and leadership, Jan aims to support CNCF's mission and strengthen the global cloud-native ecosystem.

person.name: Nemanja Filipović
person.bio: Nemanja has been spending a good part of his life exploring how technology and psychology shape collaboration. With experience across many projects, companies, and roles, he is deeply engaged with enterprise solutions, CNCF technologies, and people-centered leadership.

person.name: Sai Pisey
person.bio: I am a DevOps and Cloud Engineer specializing in Kubernetes, Docker, and Terraform, with experience deploying secure and scalable applications across AWS, Azure, and Oracle Cloud. I love automating everything that can be scripted and optimizing what others overlook. A tech-savvy problem solver at heart, I turn complex cloud challenges into clean, efficient solutions.

person.name: Viet Hung Nguyen
person.bio: I am a systems and infrastructure engineer, completely charmed by Kubernetes' ability to turn complex, distributed systems into elegant infrastructure. Discovering CNCF projects like CAPI, Metal3, and Talos Linux was a turning point for me, revealing how to build a seamless, GitOps-driven bridge between raw physical hardware and the cloud-native environment. I am now enthusiastically applying this infrastructure stack to the telecom industry's recent open-source movement, specifically focusing on running hardware-accelerated 5G O-CU and O-DU workloads entirely on Kubernetes.

person.name: Christopher Adigun
person.bio: As a Cloud Native Telco Architect, I specialize in designing telecommunications networks using Kubernetes and related software.My expertise includes advanced Kubernetes networking solutions like Multus, Whereabouts, SRIOV-Device-Plugin, Spider-Pool, and Userplane Acceleration (DPDK).I'm passionate about sharing my knowledge and have created a Udemy course on Kubernetes networking (https://www.udemy.com/course/kubernetes-baremetal-networking-using-gns3).Additionally, I create content about Kubernetes-related topics on my Medium blog (https://medium.com/@futuredon) and YouTube (https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCuAdwhfPswT1hDezzRGnTKg)

person.name: Lalam Srinu
person.bio: I'm a DevOps engineer transitioning my career toward Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. I'm passionate about open-source tools like Docker, Kubernetes, and Prometheus, and I'm actively building hands-on experience in this space.

person.name: Mateus Gabriel Da Silva
person.bio: DevOps Engineer and SRE with extensive experience in Cloud environments, focusing on Kubernetes, Linux, Observability, and major cloud providers (AWS, OCI, and GCP). Skilled in building modern, scalable, and automated infrastructures by combining best practices in CI/CD, IaC, and security to ensure high availability and operational efficiency.

person.name: Ashtuosh Kandpal
person.bio: Ashutosh Kandpal is a Dehradun-based technology consultant, Microsoft-certified trainer, and self-taught programmer specializing in cloud-native and CNCF technologies. As the organizer of CNCG Dehradun, Ashutosh leads online and offline CNCF meetups, fostering innovation and community collaboration. He is a Kubestronaut, Azure cloud certified, and a course creator for platforms like KodeKloud, passionately bridging technology and social impact.

person.name: Daniel D M
person.bio: Meet a Cloud Architect who writes code with the enthusiasm of a kid in a candy store. Armed with coffee and an insatiable appetite for automation, I transform mundane tasks into elegant solutions while Kubernetes keeps me humble with its delightful complexities.  As a devoted servant of Jesus Christ, I approach technology with the same dedication as my faith. When not wrestling with cloud infrastructure, you'll find me exploring new technologies and tools, always curious to learn what's next in the tech world. I embrace a minimalist lifestyle, finding joy in simplicity both in code and in life.  My love affair with Kubernetes stems from its beautiful chaos - where community-driven solutions tackle real-world problems head-on. Like a digital shepherd, I guide applications through the cloud, learning and growing alongside an amazing community of problem solvers.  Think of me as your friendly neighbourhood automation enthusiast, fueled by coffee and powered by faith, always ready to transform complex challenges into elegant solutions. Because in the end, technology is not just about code - it's about building bridges and growing together.  Life's journey isn't just about conquering technical challenges; it's about leaving footprints of kindness and knowledge for others to follow. After all, the greatest legacy we can leave behind is not in the systems we build, but in the lives we touch through sharing and caring.  Personal Blog: https://bookofdaniel.in/

person.name: Hou, Po-Lin
person.bio: Starting my career as a PHP Developer, I later transitioned into an SRE role through a fortunate opportunity. This transition not only broadened my technical horizon but also sparked a deep interest in exploring scalable systems and operational reliability. Along the way, through both work experience and self-study, I have been exposed to a variety of tools and skills, including CI/CD pipelines, container orchestration, and monitoring systems. I am constantly driven by a passion for cloud computing, cybersecurity, and mastering innovative technologies that push the boundaries of what is possible. My approach is guided by a curiosity for solving complex problems and a commitment to building robust, efficient systems.  Feel free to connect with me to discuss technology and share insights on technical challenges!

person.name: Jana Vonšák

When things go wrong (and they usually do), I just count it as part of the adventure. Outside the cluster, you’ll probably catch me laughing at Kubernetes memes.

person.name: Yu Kaizuka

person.name: Abdul Shukor
person.bio: DevOps Engineer with a strong passion for Kubernetes.

person.name: Egzon Demaj

I began learning more about Servers, Containers, CI/CD, K8s, Observability etc. I helped building and maintaining a government institution's Private Cloud Infrastructure where I was learning more and more about DevOps, before joining Zühlke as a DevOps Engineer and ultimately becoming a Kubestronaut.

person.name: Francis Lance Arzadon

Recently, I shifted my focus to new areas of the software development lifecycle, particularly in building and delivering stable, secure, and sustainable architectures. I believe Kubernetes is one of the most significant technological advancements of our generation, providing invaluable support to organizations across diverse industries—an inspiration for my Kubestronaut journey.

person.name: Julia Furst Morgado
person.bio: Julia is passionate about helping teams build reliable, observable, and scalable systems. At Dash0, she focuses on bridging the gap between technology and people, enabling organizations to understand their systems better and make data-driven decisions with confidence. Beyond her work, Julia is a CNCF Ambassador, AWS Container Hero, Docker Captain, Google Women Techmakers Ambassador, and Girl Code Ambassador. She’s an active community leader who helps organize Kubernetes Community Days New York, AWS Community Day New York, and the CNCF Midtown Meetup NYC, creating spaces where technologists can connect, learn, and grow together.

person.name: Paolo Patierno
person.bio: I am a Senior Principal Software Engineer working at IBM on messaging and data streaming technologies. One of these is Strimzi, a CNCF project for running Apache Kafka on Kubernetes in a cloud native manner using operators. I am also a committer on a number of IoT related projects and interested in observability and metrics – as a side project I look at how Formula 1 telemetry can be analysed with the technologies I work with every day. I am based in Napoli, where I am the lead organiser of the local CNCF Meetup.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I feel that I am one of the lucky people who love their job because it's also their passion. Taking this passion to the community by sharing my knowledge about open source technologies is something which excites me. To be a CNCF Ambassador takes this love to the next level, being even more engaged within the cloud native space.

person.name: Sigurd Hansen

When I’m not working, you’ll find me spending time with my family or refurbishing my mountain-cabin—it’s my perfect escape and a labor of love.

person.name: Chris Aniszczyk

His work is focused on working with developer community to advance open source projects at scale. Previously Chris served as Twitter's head of open source, where he led a team of developer advocates and was responsible for Twitter's open source engineering, strategy and culture. He has also contributed to Gentoo Linux and served on the Eclipse Foundation's Board of Directors and the Java Community Process Executive Committee. An engineer by trade, Chris brings a passion for both open source and community development to the organization.
